high.title: TSMC Advances to 2nm Mass Production, Taiwan Maintains 85% of Global Advanced Processes
high.summary: TSMC continues expanding advanced processes in Taiwan with 2nm technology ramping up at Kaohsiung Fab 22, while also building a new 2nm fab in Hsinchu Science Park. Industry assessment indicates that over the next 5 years, nearly 80% of TSMC's advanced processes will remain in Taiwan. TSMC's CoWoS advanced packaging monthly capacity is expected to grow from 70,000 wafers at end-2025 to 100,000 wafers by end-2026, reaching 130,000 wafers by end-2027. This drives strong demand for semiconductor backend testing and advanced packaging, benefiting Taiwan's testing houses including ASE Group, ChipMOS, and Sigurd Microelectronics, as well as testing interface firms Chunghwa Precision and MPI.

high.title: Central Bank to Hold Q1 Board Meeting on March 19, Focus on Selective Credit Controls
high.summary: Mega Financial Chairman Tony Shen, who also serves as CBC board member, supports maintaining the seventh wave of selective credit controls. He stated that policy measures are still taking effect and should continue. Real estate lending has normalized to mostly owner-occupied properties with 90% of loans being for self-use. The policy has successfully stabilized housing prices while maintaining reasonable transaction volumes. This has significant implications for Taiwan's monetary policy direction and banking sector credit allocation.

high.title: Memory Giant Nanya Tech Announces Q2 DRAM Prices to "Jump" Higher
high.summary: Nanya Technology CEO Pei-Ing Lee stated that memory supply-demand imbalance will persist with Q1 prices rising monthly and Q2 seeing "jump" increases. The tight supply situation is expected to continue until at least 2H 2028. AI server DRAM share is rapidly climbing from 4% in 2024 to 7% in 2025, potentially exceeding 10% going forward. HBM demand is consuming about 25% of production capacity despite representing only 10% of bit demand. This supply constraint combined with surging AI demand creates a powerful tailwind for memory manufacturers including Nanya Tech, Winbond, and Macronix.

high.title: US-Iran War Escalates, Hormuz Strait Closed, Global Shipping Disrupted
high.summary: Iran Revolutionary Guard commander announced closure of Hormuz Strait, threatening to fire on any vessels attempting passage. About 10% of global container fleet is affected according to ONE CEO. Major shipping lines MSC, Yang Ming, and Wan Hai have suspended Middle East cargo bookings. This closure impacts approximately 20% of global oil and LNG transportation, creating significant supply chain disruptions and driving up freight rates. Taiwan shipping companies including Evergreen, Yang Ming, and Wan Hai are benefiting from higher container rates but face operational challenges.

medium.title: Foreign Investors Sell NT$947 Billion but Buy Yang Ming Maritime 45,000 Shares
medium.summary: Despite massive foreign selling pressure of NT$947 billion amid Middle East conflicts, foreign investors notably bought Yang Ming Maritime as their top pick with 45,033 shares, anticipating shipping rate increases from Hormuz Strait closure. Other shipping stocks also saw foreign buying interest as geopolitical tensions boost freight demand.

medium.title: MediaTek, Qualcomm Battle for 6G at MWC 2026
medium.summary: At MWC 2026, MediaTek showcased world's first 6G wireless interoperability achievements and "Personal Device Cloud" technology. Qualcomm announced partnerships to accelerate 6G development with commercial systems expected from 2029. ASUS is listed among Qualcomm's 6G collaboration partners, positioning Taiwan companies in next-generation wireless infrastructure.

medium.title: NVIDIA Invests $4 Billion in Optical Communications Companies
medium.summary: NVIDIA announced $2 billion investments each in Lumentum Holdings and Coherent, totaling $4 billion to expand US manufacturing and R&D capabilities. This drives co-packaged optics (CPO) adoption as AI infrastructure scales. Taiwan optical component suppliers including GaAs Labs, Optoma Technology, and Wah Hong Industrial benefit from the supply chain expansion.

medium.title: Taiwan Stock Index Plunges 1,494 Points on Middle East War Escalation
medium.summary: TAIEX dropped 1,494 points to 32,828 as Middle East conflicts intensify. TSMC fell below 1,900 level while memory stocks hit limit down. Investment advisors view the monthly line and 33,000 level as bottom area for potential range trading. Four sectors recommended: CPO, low earth orbit satellites, AI, and defense.

medium.title: Chunghwa Precision Reports Record January Revenue of NT$9 Billion
medium.summary: Taiwan's January export orders hit record high of $76.91 billion, up 60.1% YoY for 12th consecutive month of growth. Server-related companies including Chunghwa Precision, which reported record January revenue of NT$9 billion, benefit from structural AI demand driving semiconductor testing and advanced packaging requirements.

not_relevant: Articles about food safety regulations, lottery results, cultural events, personal profiles, tourism impacts, and other topics not directly related to Taiwan financial markets, insurance sector, or monetary policy.
